subject: math
name_zh: IB DP 数学 · AI（应用与解释）
name_en: IB DP Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation
description: IB DP Math AI focuses on applied mathematics with technology (GDC). 5 topics: Number & Algebra, Functions, Geometry & Trigonometry, Statistics & Probability, Calculus. Heavy use of modelling.

domains.name: 数与代数 / Number & Algebra / Number & Algebra

nodes.name: 指数/科学计数法/近似
curriculum_points: Use and interpret scientific notation, decimal approximations, and significant figures to represent and communicate quantitative information accurately (AO2).
Apply rounding conventions and percentage error calculations to assess the reasonableness of solutions in applied contexts.

nodes.name: 等差与等比数列
curriculum_points: Model real-world situations using arithmetic and geometric sequences and series, including applications in finance and growth (AO3).
Solve problems involving the nth term and the sum of n terms for arithmetic and geometric sequences.

nodes.name: 金融数学（复利/年金）
curriculum_points: Solve problems in financial mathematics involving compound interest, annuities, amortization, and depreciation using appropriate technology (AO3).
Interpret and calculate the real purchasing power of money, considering inflation, in financial decision-making.

nodes.name: 指数与对数
curriculum_points: Manipulate and solve exponential and logarithmic equations to model growth and decay phenomena, such as population growth and radioactive decay (AO2).
Sketch and interpret graphs of exponential and logarithmic functions, identifying key features such as asymptotes and intercepts.

nodes.name: 矩阵（HL）
curriculum_points: Perform operations with matrices, including addition, scalar multiplication, and matrix multiplication, and calculate determinants and inverses of 2x2 and 3x3 matrices (AO1).
Use matrices to represent and solve systems of linear equations, and apply them to transformations in two and three dimensions.

nodes.name: 一次/二次函数
curriculum_points: Solve systems of linear equations and quadratic equations, both analytically and graphically, and interpret the solutions in context (AO2).
Analyze and model relationships between variables using linear and quadratic functions, determining key features such as vertex and axis of symmetry.

nodes.name: 指数/三角模型
curriculum_points: Construct and interpret exponential and trigonometric models (sine and cosine) to represent periodic or growth/decay behavior in real-world data (AO3).
Use trigonometric models to solve problems involving periodic phenomena such as tides, seasonal temperatures, or harmonic motion.

nodes.name: 回归与拟合
curriculum_points: Use technology to perform linear, quadratic, exponential, and sinusoidal regression on bivariate data and interpret the parameters in context (AO3).
Assess the validity of a model by analyzing residuals and the coefficient of determination (R²).

nodes.name: Logistic 模型（HL）
curriculum_points: Understand the characteristics of the logistic function and its use in modeling growth with a limiting factor or carrying capacity (AO3).
Fit a logistic model to data using technology and interpret the parameters, including the carrying capacity and point of inflection.

domains.name: 几何与三角 / Geometry & Trig / Geometry & Trig

nodes.name: 三维几何
curriculum_points: Solve problems involving points, lines, and planes in three dimensions using vector and Cartesian forms (AO2).
Calculate angles between lines and planes, and distances from a point to a line or plane in three-dimensional space.

nodes.name: Voronoi 图
curriculum_points: Construct and interpret Voronoi diagrams from a given set of sites, and determine equations of edges and vertices (AO2).
Apply Voronoi diagrams to solve optimization problems, such as finding the nearest neighbor or locating a new facility.

nodes.name: 三角应用
curriculum_points: Apply trigonometric ratios and the sine and cosine rules to solve practical problems involving non-right-angled triangles, including navigation and surveying (AO3).
Use trigonometric functions to model and solve problems involving areas of triangles and lengths of arcs and sectors.

nodes.name: 图论（HL）
curriculum_points: Understand and use terminology of graph theory, including vertices, edges, adjacency, and weight, and represent networks using adjacency matrices (AO1).
Apply algorithms, such as Kruskal's and Dijkstra's, to solve problems related to minimum spanning trees and shortest paths in weighted graphs.

domains.name: 统计与概率 / Stats & Probability / Stats & Prob

nodes.name: 描述统计
curriculum_points: Calculate, interpret, and compare descriptive statistics for univariate data, including measures of central tendency (mean, median, mode) and dispersion (range, interquartile range, variance, standard deviation) (AO2).
Construct and analyze box-and-whisker plots and cumulative frequency graphs to summarize and compare data sets.

nodes.name: 相关与回归
curriculum_points: Calculate and interpret the Pearson’s product-moment correlation coefficient (r) and the equation of the regression line y on x (AO2).
Make predictions using the regression line and understand the dangers of extrapolation and the distinction between correlation and causation.

nodes.name: 卡方检验
curriculum_points: Set up and perform a chi-squared test for independence, including calculating expected frequencies, degrees of freedom, and the p-value (AO3).
Interpret the result of a chi-squared test in context, understanding the meaning of statistical significance at a given level.

nodes.name: 正态分布
curriculum_points: Use the normal distribution to model continuous data, calculate probabilities using technology, and work with standardized values (z-scores) (AO2).
Apply the normal distribution to solve problems in context, including inverse normal calculations to find unknown mean or standard deviation.

nodes.name: 马尔可夫链（HL）
curriculum_points: Represent transition states using matrices and use Markov chains to model probabilistic systems that change over time (AO3).
Find steady-state vectors and interpret their meaning in the context of long-term behavior of the system.

nodes.name: 变化率
curriculum_points: Interpret the derivative as an instantaneous rate of change in applied contexts, such as velocity, acceleration, and marginal cost (AO2).
Estimate rates of change from tables and graphs, and use difference quotients to approximate derivatives.

nodes.name: 基本导数
curriculum_points: Differentiate functions of the form f(x) = ax^n, sin x, cos x, e^x, and ln x, and apply rules for sums and constant multiples (AO1).
Use differentiation to find equations of tangents and normals, and to identify intervals where a function is increasing or decreasing.

nodes.name: 积分入门
curriculum_points: Integrate functions of the form f(x) = ax^n, e^x, sin x, and cos x, and apply the fundamental theorem of calculus (AO1).
Use definite integration to calculate areas under curves and between curves, interpreting the result in an applied context.

nodes.name: 微分方程（HL）
curriculum_points: Formulate and solve first order differential equations using separation of variables and the integrating factor method (AO3).
Model real-world phenomena, such as Newton's Law of Cooling or simple population models, using first order differential equations and interpret the solutions.
